content: Install script best practices: (1) Detect OS via 'uname -s | tr upper lower', map variants (Darwin->macos, Linux->linux). (2) Detect arch via 'uname -m', map x86_64->amd64, aarch64->arm64. (3) Check curl then fallback to wget. (4) Download binary from GitHub Releases API: GET /repos/:owner/:repo/releases/latest, parse browser_download_url for platform asset. (5) Verify SHA256 checksums alongside each release asset. (6) Check for existing install: 'command -v claudebar' pattern + version compare. (7) Use set -euo pipefail + trap for cleanup. (8) Curl-pipe-bash security: acceptable industry norm for developer tools; mitigate by printing download URL, offering checksum verification, recommending HTTPS pinning. Grype, code-server, many major tools all use this pattern.
